user_query:
Hi! I’ve got a late-night group critique coming up and I want to assemble a small “Critique Prep Packet” from my local logs and peer notes. Please use the files under input/ and produce the following outputs under outputs/:

1) Compute metrics by running a local script or command-line program and save the results to outputs/metrics.json. The script must read both input/work_log.csv and input/feedback.jsonl and the JSON must include these fields:
- minutes_by_block: an object mapping each time-of-day block to the total minutes (ints)
- avg_focus_by_block: an object mapping each block to the average focus score rounded to 2 decimals
- theme_counts: an object mapping each feedback theme to its total count (ints)
- sentiment_counts: an object with keys positive, neutral, negative and their counts (ints)
- top_themes: an array of exactly the top 3 themes sorted by count desc, then alphabetically on ties; each item with {"theme": string, "count": int}
- primary_block: the block with the highest total minutes (alphabetical tie-breaker)
Also create outputs/commands_run.txt containing the exact command(s) you used to run the script (e.g., python scripts/compute_metrics.py … or a shell one-liner). I need this step to be reproducible.

2) Prepare meeting notes in outputs/meeting_notes.md that I can bring to critique. The document must include these four section headings exactly: Attendees, Agenda, Key Feedback Themes, Action Items.
- Attendees: list everyone from input/critique_agenda.yaml plus me ("Me").
- Agenda: include every goal from input/critique_agenda.yaml as bullet points, and add one bullet that says: "Data check: Peak work block is <primary_block> with <minutes> min; avg focus <avg>" using the numbers from outputs/metrics.json (avg rounded to 2 decimals).
- Key Feedback Themes: list the top 3 themes from outputs/metrics.json as "<theme> – <count> comments".
- Action Items: write at least one actionable item per top theme. Each item should start with an action verb, include the theme in brackets, and include the comment count in parentheses, e.g., "Refine transition storyboard [story clarity] (3 comments)". Add a short "Done when:" line for each item with a concrete completion criterion.

3) Draft a concise project write-up in outputs/project_summary.md (250–300 words) describing my nocturnal comic/illustration project. Work in at least two data points from outputs/metrics.json: reference the primary_block with its total minutes and its average focus score (rounded to 2 decimals). Also mention the highest-frequency feedback theme by name and count (use alphabetical tie-breaker). Keep the tone practical but creative so it reads like a real artist statement for critique prep.

Inputs:
- input/work_log.csv
- input/feedback.jsonl
- input/critique_agenda.yaml

Please do not use the internet. Make sure the meeting notes reflect the agenda participants and goals from the YAML file, and ensure all numbers in the notes and summary come from outputs/metrics.json. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
